vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The registry is largely non-representative of current biological reality.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: Yellow pot is present but contains no visible plant matter.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Mexican Mint.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: Pothos is absent; only the white rabbit anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Silver Guest.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil, no signs of life.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present, but the host plant (Pothos) has been removed or perished.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil, no signs of life.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dry and inert across all containers.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of eggshell fragments in the central black pot; a small electronic component (IC chip/header) appears in the later images.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The desk surface has been cleared of most organic material, leaving only the empty pots and the rabbit anchor.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Gradual removal of organic matter; the Pothos leaf in the black pot shows signs of wilting and eventual disappearance.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_CURRENT: Total loss of remaining foliage. Introduction of a small electronic component (IC chip) near the pots.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is effectively defunct. There is zero active photosynthesis occurring. The environment has transitioned from a botanical display to a collection of empty vessels and non-biological debris.
anomalies: Introduction of an electronic component (IC chip) in the final stages.
Complete disappearance of all cataloged plant specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a complete collapse of the indoor garden. Over the 5-day observation period, the specimens have been systematically removed or have perished. The current state is characterized by empty, dry pots and the presence of inorganic debris, suggesting the space has been repurposed or abandoned as a botanical site.
